summary: Platform limitation research for zuicchini (winit 0.30 + wgpu on Linux). Four issues investigated with tier-ranked solutions.

issues.recommended_solution: Use CursorGrabMode::Locked + DeviceEvent::MouseMotion as primary mechanism (Wayland), with set_cursor_position warp loop as X11 fallback. Attempt Locked first; if it returns ExternalError::NotSupported, fall back to warp-based approach.

wayland: DeviceEvent::MouseMotion { delta: (dx, dy) } for relative deltas

issues.wayland_behavior: CursorGrabMode::Locked works. Uses zwp_pointer_constraints_v1 to freeze cursor, zwp_relative_pointer_v1 delivers unaccelerated deltas via DeviceEvent::MouseMotion. Both protocols are in wayland-protocols/unstable but universally supported (GNOME, KDE, wlroots).
issues.x11_behavior: CursorGrabMode::Locked returns ExternalError::NotSupported. set_cursor_position works via XWarpPointer. Standard warp-each-frame pattern matches emCore's C++ approach.
issues.crates_needed_note: No new crates. winit 0.30 provides all required APIs: set_cursor_grab, set_cursor_position, set_cursor_visible, DeviceEvent::MouseMotion.
caveats: X11 warp generates synthetic CursorMoved events that must be filtered out (detect warp target position and discard).
zwp_pointer_constraints_v1 is still in wayland-protocols unstable/ but all major compositors implement it.
set_cursor_position on Wayland without lock returns NotSupported (by design).
DeviceEvent::MouseMotion deltas may include acceleration when pointer is not locked; during lock, winit uses unaccelerated values.
alternatives_rejected: set_cursor_position warp loop everywhere: cannot work on Wayland (returns NotSupported unless pointer already locked).
CursorGrabMode::Confined only: cursor still moves visually, defeats stick-mouse UX.
Direct Wayland protocol crates (wayland-client, smithay-client-toolkit): winit already wraps the protocols; parallel binding would conflict.
Third-party crates (mouse-rs): for synthetic input injection, not pointer lock.

issues.tier_label: Single-platform only (Linux, no macOS/Windows but that's our only target)
issues.recommended_solution: dlopen libcanberra at runtime, call ca_context_play with event.id='bell-terminal'. Uses freedesktop Sound Naming Specification. Graceful no-op if libcanberra not installed.
issues.wayland_behavior: No Wayland protocol for system bell. libcanberra works on Wayland because it talks directly to PulseAudio/PipeWire via its own backend modules, not through the display server. GTK4's gdk_wayland_display_beep is a no-op; libcanberra is the correct path.
issues.x11_behavior: XBell generates XKB bell events intercepted by PulseAudio's module-x11-bell. libcanberra is strictly better: works on both X11 and Wayland, plays user-configured sound theme, no dependency on PulseAudio's X11 interception module.

crates_needed.name: libloading
crates_needed.purpose: Safe dlopen/dlsym wrappers for runtime libcanberra loading

caveats: libcanberra is 'considered obsolete' but has no successor for event sounds. Every major Linux desktop still ships it.
ca_context_play is varargs in C; Rust FFI requires extern "C" fn(...) with c_str pointers terminated by null.
ca_context_play is async (returns immediately, sound plays in background). Desirable for UI beep.
If libcanberra not installed, dlopen fails gracefully → silent no-op. Log at debug level.
On NixOS/Guix, the .so path may differ from standard libcanberra.so.0.
alternatives_rejected: actually_beep crate (0.1.1): synthesized sine wave, not system alert sound. Pulls in full cpal audio stack.
\x07 BEL character: only works in terminal emulators, not GUI apps.
Direct XBell via x11rb: X11-only, no Wayland support.
winit API: winit 0.30 exposes no bell/alert API.
Ghostty approach (gdk_display_beep via GTK): requires GTK linkage, Wayland implementation is a no-op.
Alacritty approach (shell out to canberra-gtk-play): fragile, requires external binary.

issues.recommended_solution: D-Bus org.freedesktop.ScreenSaver.Inhibit via zbus blocking API as primary mechanism. Optional x11rb XScreenSaverSuspend fallback for standalone X screensavers (xscreensaver).
issues.wayland_behavior: GNOME (gnome-session) and KDE (kscreenlocker) implement org.freedesktop.ScreenSaver D-Bus. wlroots compositors (Sway, Hyprland) do NOT implement it — they use zwp_idle_inhibit_manager_v1 Wayland protocol instead, bridgeable via xdg-desktop-portal-wlr/hyprland. D-Bus call silently fails if no service is listening.
issues.x11_behavior: GNOME and KDE on X11 implement org.freedesktop.ScreenSaver D-Bus. Standalone xscreensaver does NOT — fallback to XScreenSaverSuspend (MIT-SCREEN-SAVER extension) via x11rb (already a transitive winit dependency).

crates_needed.name: zbus
crates_needed.purpose: Pure Rust D-Bus client for org.freedesktop.ScreenSaver.Inhibit

caveats: wlroots compositors without xdg-desktop-portal will silently ignore D-Bus inhibit (no service listening). Acceptable — those users manage their own idle config.
XFCE uses org.xfce.ScreenSaver, MATE uses org.mate.ScreenSaver — not org.freedesktop. Could add as secondary targets if needed.
zbus 5.x is pure Rust but brings ~15-20 transitive crates including async runtime machinery.
zbus::blocking must not be called from async context. Zuicchini's synchronous winit event loop is fine.
Some KDE versions expose service at /ScreenSaver instead of /org/freedesktop/ScreenSaver. Try both or target /org/freedesktop/ScreenSaver (works on modern KDE).
Inhibition auto-releases if D-Bus connection drops (crash/exit), providing fail-safe cleanup.
alternatives_rejected: keepawake crate (0.6.0): good library but adds unnecessary abstraction for ~10 lines of D-Bus code. Zuicchini only needs Linux.
org.freedesktop.portal.Inhibit (xdg-desktop-portal): more complex (Request handle, window identifier). ScreenSaver interface is simpler for just screensaver inhibition.

zwp_idle_inhibit_manager_v1 (Wayland protocol): requires binding to Wayland display and wl_surface. winit owns the connection and doesn't expose protocol registration. Massive complexity for marginal gain.
XScreenSaverSuspend only: X11-only, cannot be primary approach.
Timer-based XResetScreenSaver (emCore approach): brute-force hack, doesn't work on Wayland.
dbus crate (0.9.10, C bindings): requires linking to libdbus-1.so. zbus is pure Rust and better maintained.

issues.recommended_solution: On X11: compute exact per-side sizes via inner_position() - outer_position(). On Wayland CSD: compute via outer_size() - inner_size() with symmetric border heuristic. On Wayland SSD: return (0,0,0,0) — correct because client surface IS the content area.

wayland_csd_fallback: dw = outer.width - inner.width; dh = outer.height - inner.height

issues.wayland_behavior: CSD: outer_size() includes sctk-adwaita decorations, inner_size() excludes them. Subtraction works. inner/outer_position() return NotSupportedError. SSD (GNOME, KDE when zxdg_decoration_manager_v1 is available): outer_size() == inner_size(), returning (0,0,0,0) is correct — compositor draws decorations outside client surface.
issues.x11_behavior: Both inner_position() and outer_position() succeed. winit internally reads _NET_FRAME_EXTENTS or climbs window hierarchy. Exact asymmetric per-side values available.
issues.crates_needed_note: No new crates. winit 0.30 provides all required APIs: outer_size, inner_size, inner_position, outer_position.
caveats: Wayland SSD is fundamentally unsolvable — compositor intentionally hides decoration geometry. But (0,0,0,0) is the correct answer: client surface IS content area, no offset needed.
Timing on X11: _NET_FRAME_EXTENTS may not be set immediately after window creation. winit caches and lazily updates.
CSD border sizes may be zero. sctk-adwaita draws title bar (~36px) but border width is effectively 0 (shadows are outside geometry). Result may be (0, 36, 0, 0).
Scale factor: outer_size/inner_size return physical pixels. Current border_sizes() returns logical-ish values — must decide coordinate space.
winit prefers SSD when compositor supports zxdg_decoration_manager_v1 (GNOME, KDE). Falls back to CSD on wlroots-based compositors (Sway, Hyprland).
alternatives_rejected: Reading _NET_FRAME_EXTENTS directly via x11rb: winit already does this internally. Duplicates functionality.
Using smithay-client-toolkit to query decoration sizes: sctk-adwaita frame dimensions not exposed via public API.
Hardcoding per-compositor values: fragile and unmaintainable.

already_available: x11rb (transitive via winit, optional screensaver_inhibition fallback)
